title: pultenaea gunnii subsp. gunnii
intro: Pultenaea gunnii subsp. gunnii, also known as the Tasmanian bush-pea, is a shrub native to Tasmania, Australia, belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues and as a natural diuretic. The Tasmanian bush-pea is valued for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, digestive issues, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications and side effects.
